response:
Nurse Emily organized a creative writing workshop for the 24 residents in her care facility. She divided them into 4 groups, with each group consisting of 6 participants. This ensured that each group received personalized attention and support. To begin the session, Emily planned to allocate 3 writing prompts per group. 

With 4 groups, that means 4 × 3 = 12 writing prompts for the initial set. To accommodate the possibility of the groups wanting to continue writing, Emily prepared an additional set of 10 extra prompts. These prompts were to be shared equally among all the groups. 

In addition to the initial prompts, Emily also made sure to have a well-stocked library of writing ideas, which she kept in the facility's library for easy access. This library was a valuable resource that encouraged creativity and provided variety for the residents. 

To find the total number of writing prompts Emily needed to prepare, we add the initial 12 prompts to the 10 extra prompts. This results in a total of 22 writing prompts. 

The END.
